skill.name: qwen-image-pro
skill.description: Generate and edit images with Alibaba Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro through the inference.sh CLI, with strong support for posters, banners, and text-heavy designs.
skill.summary: Generate professional text-heavy images with Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro through inference.sh.
skill.author: toolshell

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many shell, URL, and weak-crypto patterns, but most are markdown examples, documentation links, or substring matches in prose. The confirmed concern is that the skill allows the infsh CLI and sends prompts, dimensions, seeds, and reference image URLs to an external inference service.

medium_findings.title: External Inference CLI Sends User Content to Remote Service
medium_findings.description: The skill permits Bash(infsh *) and documents infsh app run commands for alibaba/qwen-image-2-pro. This is expected functionality, but prompts and image inputs can be transmitted to inference.sh or its provider backend, so users need a data-sharing warning.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration and CLI examples directly show external command use for remote image generation. The behavior is legitimate for the skill, but it clearly creates network data exposure.

medium_findings.title: Reference Image URLs May Expose External Resources
medium_findings.description: The image editing example accepts reference_images with URI values. This can disclose user-provided image URLs to the inference service and may cause remote fetching by the provider, depending on CLI behavior.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented input schema includes remote image URIs, but the file does not show the implementation of how infsh fetches or forwards them. The risk is credible but depends on service-side handling.

low_findings.title: Ruby Backtick Execution Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged many markdown backticks and fenced examples as Ruby shell execution. No Ruby code or dynamic backtick execution is present in SKILL.md; the real command risk is limited to the documented infsh CLI usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are markdown inline code or fenced bash examples, not Ruby source. Manual review found no Ruby execution path in the skill file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Substring False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts map to prose, model names, table headers, or prompt text. No hashing, encryption, certificate, or cryptographic API usage appears in the reviewed SKILL.md content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is documentation for image generation options and output fields. There is no cryptographic operation or weak algorithm selection to exploit.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Alerts Are Documentation and Example Links
low_findings.description: The URL findings point to inference.sh documentation, a preview image, and example.com reference images. They do not show credential exfiltration, hidden endpoints, or unauthorized network calls inside the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation or placeholder examples in markdown. The only network concern is the expected use of the inference.sh service.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alert Is a Parameter Description False Positive
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance alert points to the negative_prompt parameter row. This is an image generation option for content to avoid, not a host inspection command or environment discovery behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 105 is a markdown table entry describing negative_prompt. Manual review found no hostname, user, process, filesystem, or network reconnaissance command.

dangerous_patterns.title: Authorized External CLI Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill grants Bash access to infsh and instructs users to run alibaba/qwen-image-2-pro. External CLI execution is required for the skill, but it should remain scoped to infsh and not be expanded without review.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools metadata and examples confirm the pattern. The scope is narrow, so this is a managed risk rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

content.user_title: Generate Professional Images with Qwen Pro
content.value_statement: Text-heavy image generation often produces distorted lettering and weak layout control. This skill uses Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro through inference.sh for posters, banners, and edited images.
actual_capabilities: Runs Alibaba Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro through the inference.sh CLI.
Generates images from text prompts with configurable width and height.
Supports text-heavy posters and banners with explicit lettering instructions.
Accepts negative prompts to reduce blur, distortion, and low quality output.
Can request multiple image variations and reproducible seeds.
Documents reference-image editing workflows for style and outfit transfer.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh CLI and an authenticated account before use.
Prompts and reference image URLs may be sent to an external service.
Output quality depends on model availability, prompt clarity, and provider limits.
Generated text can still contain errors and should be reviewed before publication.

use_cases.title: Create Campaign Posters
use_cases.description: Generate posters with specified titles, subtitles, colors, and layout details for fast visual drafts.

use_cases.title: Produce Product Banners
use_cases.description: Create wide promotional banners with clear sale text, background style, and image dimensions.

use_cases.title: Prototype Image Editing Prompts
use_cases.description: Test reference-image edits, style transfer ideas, and repeatable seeds before building a larger workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Image Prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: Use qwen-image-pro to generate an image of [subject] in [style]. Use [width] by [height] pixels and avoid [negative details].

prompt_templates.title: Poster with Exact Text
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a design where readable text is important.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a poster with the exact title "[title]" and subtitle "[subtitle]". Place the title at [position], use [font style], and set prompt_extend to false.

prompt_templates.title: Marketing Banner Variations
prompt_templates.scenario: Create several options for a campaign asset.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate [number] banner variations for [campaign]. Use [brand colors], [main text], [secondary text], and a [visual style] background.

prompt_templates.title: Reference Image Edit
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced image editing with source images.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use qwen-image-pro with these reference images: [image URLs]. Edit them so [specific change], preserve [details], and generate [number] options.

output_examples.input: Generate a summer sale banner with large white headline text, yellow discount text, and a sunset background.
output_examples.output: Returns one or more generated banner images and basic output metadata such as image count and dimensions.

output_examples.input: Create a poster with the exact title GRAND OPENING and a smaller event date below it.
output_examples.output: Produces a poster-style image draft with the requested text placement, plus metadata for the generated file.

output_examples.input: Edit a person reference image to wear the outfit from a second reference image.
output_examples.output: Returns edited image variations based on the provided references and the requested visual change.

best_practices: Use exact quoted text when the image must contain readable words.
Set prompt_extend to false when precise text placement matters.
Review outputs for text accuracy, brand compliance, and external service data exposure.
anti_patterns: Do not send confidential images or private campaign text without approval for external processing.
Do not rely on generated text without human review before publication.
Do not request vague layouts when exact poster or banner composition is required.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It helps generate and edit images with Alibaba Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro through the inference.sh CLI.

faq.question: Does it require setup?
faq.answer: Yes. You need the inference.sh CLI, an authenticated account, and access to the Qwen image app.

faq.question: Is it good for text in images?
faq.answer: It is designed for stronger text rendering, but every generated word should still be checked manually.

faq.question: Can it edit existing images?
faq.answer: The skill documents reference-image workflows for edits such as style transfer and outfit changes.

faq.question: What data leaves my environment?
faq.answer: Prompts, generation settings, and reference image URLs may be sent to inference.sh or provider systems.

faq.question: Is this safe to publish?
faq.answer: It appears publishable with a warning about external CLI use and remote processing of user content.
